q: The speed of sound in air at 0°C is approximately _______.
Sound waves are classified as _______ waves.
The frequency range of audible sound for humans is _______.
Sound waves with frequencies above 20,000 Hz are called _______.
The speed of sound is fastest in _______.
The pitch of a sound is determined by its _______.
The loudness of sound depends on the _______ of the wave.
The unit of sound intensity level is the _______.
The change in frequency due to relative motion between source and observer is the _______.
When a sound source approaches a stationary observer, the observed frequency _______.
When a sound source moves away from a stationary observer, the observed frequency _______.
If both the source and observer move in the same direction at the same speed, the Doppler shift is _______.
A reflected sound wave is called an _______.
The distance between two consecutive nodes in a standing wave is _______.
The distance between a node and the adjacent antinode is _______.
Standing waves are produced by the _______ of two waves traveling in opposite directions.
Points of zero displacement in a standing wave are called _______.
An open organ pipe has _______ at both ends.
A pipe closed at one end produces only _______ harmonics.
The fundamental frequency is also known as the _______.
In an open pipe, the third harmonic is the _______ overtone.
The ratio of object speed to the speed of sound is the _______.
Shock waves are produced when an object moves _______ the speed of sound.
The loud noise caused by a shock wave is called a _______.
Beats are produced by the interference of two waves of _______ frequencies.
Resonance occurs when a driving frequency matches the _______ of the system.
The quality of sound, which allows us to distinguish between instruments, is called _______.
Echocardiography uses _______ to examine the heart.
Bats navigate using a process called _______.
Sound waves _______ around corners and obstacles.
The threshold of human hearing is defined as _______.
The threshold of pain for the human ear is approximately _______.
In a longitudinal wave, the high-pressure regions are called _______.
In a longitudinal wave, the low-pressure regions are called _______.
As the temperature of air increases, the speed of sound _______.
In a pipe closed at one end, the first overtone is the _______ harmonic.
The number of beats per second is equal to the _______ of the two frequencies.
Sound cannot travel through _______.
Ultrasonic waves can be used to detect _______ in metal castings.
A sonic boom is associated with _______ waves.
Which of the following describes the bending of waves around an obstacle?
A stationary source and an observer moving toward each other results in _______ frequency.
The fundamental frequency of an open pipe of length L is _______.
The fundamental frequency of a closed pipe of length L is _______.
The intensity of sound _______ as the distance from the source increases.
Mach 2 means the object is moving at _______ the speed of sound.
High-frequency sounds that can reveal blockages in blood flow are _______.
The period of a wave is the _______ of its frequency.
In a standing wave, points of maximum displacement are called _______.
The speed of sound in air _______ with an increase in humidity.
